titleEn: Tech Hub LIVE Returns to Des Moines July 20-22 with Practical Answers for a High-Pressure Ag Economy
titleZh: Tech Hub LIVE 2026：为高压农业经济提供实用技术答案
summaryZh: Tech Hub LIVE 2026大会将于2026年7月20-22日在爱荷华州得梅因举行，聚焦农业零售渠道技术采纳。面对投入成本高、利润压缩和贸易不确定性，大会通过从业者主导的会议、现场研究结果和同行交流，帮助参会者识别真正能带来投资回报的技术。会议涵盖AI、无人机政策、网络安全、精准农业等多个主题，并发布普渡大学精准农业调查结果。
translationZh:
CropLife分享 CroplifeTM Media Group（Meister Media Worldwide旗下品牌）宣布，农业零售渠道技术采纳领域的领先会议——Tech Hub LIVE Conference and ExpoSM（THL）——将于2026年7月20日至22日在爱荷华州得梅因举行。本次大会将汇聚60多位演讲者、50多家参展商，以及数百名面临近年来最具经济挑战性季节的农业零售商和种植者。

由于投入成本依然高企、利润空间被压缩，以及贸易不确定性重塑市场格局，农业零售商和种植者面临着越来越大的压力，需要识别那些能带来可衡量投资回报的技术，并淘汰那些无法实现这一目标的技术。2026年的THL正是围绕这一挑战而构建：从业者主导的会议、现场研究成果以及直接的同行交流，旨在帮助参会者利用现有工具做出更明智的决策。

会议议程包括全体会议、涵盖智能技术、运营、销售赋能和田间实践等主题的分组讨论，以及炉边谈话、技术讲座和引导式圆桌讨论。

**第一天**以Land O'Lakes高级副总裁兼首席技术官Teddy Bekele的主旨演讲开场，探讨人工智能对食品和农业的实际影响。

**第二天**，AWS的Karen Hildebrand将就“在人工智能时代构建韧性农业企业”发表演讲，随后将举行关于ESA合规、无人机政策、网络安全威胁、农业金融、生物技术创新以及农民视角小组讨论等并行会议。

**第三天**聚焦农场层面的决策：谷物营销、数据工作流、客户采纳和销售赋能，同时还将发布我们年度普渡大学精准农业调查结果。

“今年不是盲目试验技术的年份。利润空间太薄，风险太高，”Meister Media Worldwide旗下CropLife杂志编辑Eric Sfiligoj表示。“Tech Hub LIVE正是行业汇聚一堂，弄清楚什么真正有效，并带着计划离开的地方。”

Farm4Profit播客的Tanner Winterhof将连续三天担任主持人。

展区将汇聚50多家公司，包括AgGateway、AgVend、Agri-Access、Compass Regulatory、Ever.Ag、InnerPlant、Intelinair、Raven Industries、Spectrum Technologies和Taranis等。完整的参展商和演讲者名单可在techhublive.com上查阅。

热门文章 阅读更多 阅读更多 阅读更多 阅读更多 阅读更多 超越颠覆：通过种植者主导的创新在农业科技中赢得信任并产生影响

注册现已开放，请访问techhublive.com/register。参会者可在techhublive.com/hotel预订酒店住宿。完整会议日程
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了农业技术大会如何应对当前经济压力下的技术采纳挑战，其中AI、精准农业、数据工作流等议题与智慧畜牧行业的技术应用高度相关，可为养殖业技术选型提供参考。
contentFull: By CropLife Share CroplifeTM Media Group, a Meister Media Worldwide brand, announced that Tech Hub LIVE Conference and ExpoSM (THL), the agricultural industry’s leading conference for technology adoption in the retail channel, will take place July 20-22, 2026, in Des Moines, Iowa, drawing more than 60 speakers, more than 50 exhibitors, and hundreds of ag retailers and growers confronting one of the most economically demanding seasons in recent memory. With input costs still elevated, margins compressed, and trade uncertainty reshaping market dynamics, ag retailers and growers face mounting pressure to identify technologies that deliver measurable return on investment and to move past those that do not. THL 2026 is built around that challenge: practitioner-led sessions, live research results, and direct peer exchange designed to help attendees make better decisions with the tools available to them now. The program includes general sessions, concurrent breakouts across Smart Tech, Operations, Sales Enablement, and In the Field tracks, plus Fireside Chats, Tech Talks and facilitated roundtables. Day 1 opens with a keynote from Teddy Bekele, SVP and CTO at Land O’Lakes, on AI’s practical impact on food and agriculture. Day 2 features Karen Hildebrand of AWS on building resilient ag businesses in the age of AI, followed by concurrent sessions on ESA compliance, drone policy, cybersecurity threats, ag finance, biotech innovation and the farmer’s perspective panel. Day 3 zeroes in on farm-level decision-making: grain marketing, data workflows, customer adoption, and sales enablement along with our annual Purdue Precision Ag Survey results. “This is not a year to experiment blindly with technology. Margins are too thin and the stakes are too high,” said Eric Sfiligoj, Editor of CropLife at Meister Media Worldwide. “Tech Hub LIVE is where the industry comes to figure out what actually works and walk away with a plan.” MORE BY CROPLIFE Digital Farming June 18, 2026 9 Ways Collaboration Is Accelerating Innovation Across AgTech By CropLife|June 18, 2026 Digital Farming June 17, 2026 Beyond Disruption: Earning Trust and Driving Impact in AgTech Through Grower-Led Innovation By CropLife|June 17, 2026 Analytics March 8, 2026 EarthDaily’s Dave Gebhardt on the Future of Satellite Data in Agriculture By CropLife|March 8, 2026 Tanner Winterhof of the Farm4Profit Podcast returns as emcee across all three days. The exhibit floor features more than 50 companies including AgGateway, AgVend, Agri-Access, Compass Regulatory, Ever.Ag, InnerPlant, Intelinair, Raven Industries, Spectrum Technologies, and Taranis, among others. The full exhibitor and speaker roster is available at techhublive.com. Top Articles Read More Read More Read More Read More Read More Beyond Disruption: Earning Trust and Driving Impact in AgTech Through Grower-Led Innovation Registration is open at techhublive.com/register. Attendees can book hotel accommodations at techhublive.com/hotel. author: Matt Hopkins
contentHtml:
Join ag retail’s premier technology conference for data-driven insights, peer networking, and innovative solutions shaping the future of agriculture.
The post Tech Hub LIVE Returns to Des Moines July 20-22 with Practical Answers for a High-Pressure Ag Economy appeared first on Global Ag Tech Initiative.
